title: cornus amomum subsp. obliqua
intro: Cornus amomum subsp. obliqua, also known as the Chinese dogwood, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Cornaceae family. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antiseptic effects, which help to reduce fever, relieve pain, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Cornus amomum subsp. obliqua include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and its roots and berries are used in traditional herbal preparations to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
